dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini had the strongest positive HN vibe today: Gemma 4 drew hands-on interest and favorable performance comparisons, while Anthropic mentions skewed toward Claude bugs and shaky Claude Code output [E1][E2][E3][E4].
winnerExplanation: Gemini wins because it had the only clearly positive recurring signal across multiple threads. A dedicated Gemma 4 setup story produced practical, generally favorable reports about speed and usefulness on Mac hardware despite some caveats [E1], and a separate local-AI thread explicitly contrasted Apple’s bundled model unfavorably with Gemma 4 [E2]. Anthropic, by contrast, was discussed mainly through complaints about Claude burning tokens and Claude Code being associated with inaccurate vibe-coded trackers [E3][E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Negative day. Claude was mentioned mainly for a bug that reportedly burned through tokens quickly, and Claude Code was blamed for inaccurate tracker sites in a separate thread [E3][E4].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Mildly positive day. Gemma 4 got substantive HN experimentation and generally favorable local-use impressions, and elsewhere it was cited as ahead of Apple’s built-in model, though some launch-day bugs and tool-calling issues were noted [E1][E2].

evidence.summary: Story about setting up Ollama with Gemma 4 on a Mac mini. Comments include favorable hands-on reports about speed and usefulness for coding tasks, alongside cautions about early bugs and tool-calling issues.
In the Apfel local-AI thread, a commenter said Apple’s built-in model lagged behind newer small open models and specifically named Gemma 4 as a notable improvement.
The Apfel thread included a complaint about a 'Claude bug' burning through tokens quickly, with the commenter saying it pushed them to try alternatives and local models instead.
In the Artemis II tracker Show HN, a commenter said inaccurate tracker clones 'obviously used Claude Code,' directly tying Anthropic’s coding assistant to poor output quality.
